response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings genuine geospatial interest through his GISCollective platform and has distributed-systems exposure from high-throughput microservices work at Sociomantic, but the role's primary technical requirement — strong, current C++ or Rust — is essentially unmet. His C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project; Rust does not appear anywhere on the resume. His career trajectory has been firmly in JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no evidence of routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or traffic-signal integration.
strengths: Founded and maintains GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform, demonstrating genuine geospatial domain knowledge and long-term commitment
Hands-on distributed-systems experience at Sociomantic building low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Polyglot background (DLang, C++, Node.js, Java, Linux) signals adaptability and willingness to work outside a single stack
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old; Rust is entirely absent — the role's core technical requirement is not met
No experience with routing tile generation, navigation pipelines, map-data transformation, or real-time traffic integration
Currently on an open-ended career gap since October 2025 with no stated reason, and recent roles were exclusively TypeScript/Node.js product work
